person.name: Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem

nameForms.fullText: Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem

burialPlace.address: Church of the Crutched Friars, City of London, England, United Kingdom

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Father Sir William le Botiller, 3rd Lord le Botiller of Wem and Oversley, b. c 1331, d. 14 Aug 1369

Mother Elizabeth de Handesacre, d. a May 1361

Elizabeth le Botiller was born circa 1345 at of Wemme, Whixall, Hinstock, & Loppington, Shropshire, England; Age 24 in 1369.

She and Sir Robert de Ferrers, 1st Baron Ferrers of Wemme obtained a marriage license on 27 September 1369 at Acton Burnell, Shropshire, England; They had 1 son (Sir Robert, 2nd Lord Ferrers).

Elizabeth le Botiller married Sir John Say before 24 November 1381.

Elizabeth le Botiller married Sir Thomas Molington before 25 October 1398; Date of Papal indult.

Elizabeth le Botiller died on 19 June 1411; Requested to be buried in the Church of the Crutched Friars next to the Tower of London.

«b»Family 1«/b»
Sir Robert de Ferrers, 1st Baron Ferrers of Wemme b. c 1350, d. 31 Dec 1380

«b»Child«/b»
1.) Sir Robert de Ferrers, 2nd Lord Ferrers of Wem b. c 1372, d. b 29 Nov 1396

«b»Family 2«/b»
Sir John Say d. bt 5 Jul 1395-25 Oct 1398

«b»Family 3«/b»
Sir Thomas Molington d. 7 May 1408

«b»Burial«/b»
"The only recorded aristocratic memorials at the Crutched Friars were for the family of Elizabeth Botiller, baroness de Wemme (d. 1411). In her will, Lady de Wemme bequeathed £6 for the building of the cloister at the Crutched Friars: this is in fact the only evidence of an aristocratic benefactor for this London house. Tombs were recorded there for her, her third husband Sir Thomas de Molington (d. 1408), her son Robert Ferers (d. 1396) and a grandson Lionel. Robert and Lionel were recorded as 'de Moligton' but this is an error because the baroness did not have any children by her third husband. It is likely that a hasty recording of the inscription led to the mistaken understanding that her son and grandson were 'de Molington' and not 'Ferers'. At her death, the baroness' co-heirs were her granddaughters, Elizabeth and Mary Ferers and it seems that their brother Lionel had predeceased his grandmother. In her will, Lady Wemme asked to be buried in the Lady Chapel where, it is thought, her tomb and those for her family were placed."

zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem於1345年出生在英國英格蘭史洛普爾維姆時，她的父親William Le Boteler Third Baron of Wemme and Oversley51歲，母親Elizabeth de Handesacre30歲。她於1369年9月27日與Sir Robert Ferrers 1st Baron Ferrers of Wem在英國史洛普爾阿克頓 伯奈爾結婚。她去世於1411年6月19日在英國英格蘭享年66歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem was born in 1345, in Wem, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, William Le Boteler Third Baron of Wemme and Oversley, was 51 and her mother, Elizabeth de Handesacre, was 30. She married Sir Robert Ferrers 1st Baron Ferrers of Wem on 27 September 1369, in Acton Burnell, Shropshire, England. She died on 19 June 1411, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 66, and was buried in London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem est née en 1345, à Wem, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Le Boteler Third Baron of Wemme and Oversley, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de Handesacre, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Robert Ferrers 1st Baron Ferrers of Wem le 27 septembre 1369, à Acton Burnell, Shropshire, Angleterre. Elle est décédée le 19 juin 1411, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 66 ans, et a été enterrée à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem 1345 in Wem, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Le Boteler Third Baron of Wemme and Oversley 51 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth de Handesacre 30. Sie hat am 27. September 1369 in Acton Burnell, Shropshire, England Sir Robert Ferrers 1st Baron Ferrers of Wem geheiratet. Sie starb am 19. Juni 1411 im Alter von 66 Jahren in England, United Kingdom und wurde in London, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem è nata il nel 1345 nel Wem, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, William Le Boteler Third Baron of Wemme and Oversley, aveva 51 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth de Handesacre, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Sir Robert Ferrers 1st Baron Ferrers of Wem il 27 settembre 1369, nel Acton Burnell, Shropshire, England. Morì il 19 giugno 1411, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 66 anni, e fu sepolta a London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wemが1345年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアウエムで生まれた時，父William Le Boteler Third Baron of Wemme and Oversleyは51歳，母Elizabeth de Handesacreは30歳であった。1369年9月27日にイギリスシュロプシュアイアアクトン ブルネルでSir Robert Ferrers 1st Baron Ferrers of Wemと結婚した。彼女は1411年6月19日にイギリスイギリスで66歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem(이)가 1345년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 w에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Le Boteler Third Baron of Wemme and Oversley(은)는 51세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth de Handesacre(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1369년 9월 27일 영국 슈럽실르 악턴 버넬에서 Sir Robert Ferrers 1st Baron Ferrers of Wem(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1411년 6월 19일 영국 잉글랜드에서 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem nasceu em 1345, em Wem, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William Le Boteler Third Baron of Wemme and Oversley, tinha 51 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth de Handesacre, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Robert Ferrers 1st Baron Ferrers of Wem em 27 de setembro de 1369, em Acton Burnell, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Ela faleceu em 19 de junho de 1411, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 66 anos, e foi sepultada em London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem родилась в 1345 г. в местности вем, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени William Le Boteler Third Baron of Wemme and Oversley было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Handesacre, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Robert Ferrers 1st Baron Ferrers of Wem 27 сентября 1369 г. в местности актон бурнелл, шропшир, Англия. Она умерла 19 июня 1411 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 66 лет и была похоронена в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem nació en 1345, en Wem, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William Le Boteler Third Baron of Wemme and Oversley, tenía 51 años y su mamá, Elizabeth de Handesacre, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Sir Robert Ferrers 1st Baron Ferrers of Wem el 27 de septiembre de 1369, en Acton Burnell, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Murió el 19 de junio de 1411, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 66 años, y fue sepultada en London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
